verdict: 2026-07-21 22:13 PDT — The plan-stage architecture is sound overall, and all round 1–4 decisions remain closed. One lifecycle gap keeps it from being fully ready: the date-only canonical path can silently replace an already approved plan.

findings.title: Date-only artifact identity can overwrite an approved plan
findings.claim: Every invocation writes `reviews/audit-plan-<YYYY-MM-DD>.json`, with no plan identifier or collision policy. Consult edits legitimately regenerate the current proposal, but a separate invocation on the same day targets the identical canonical path and can silently replace an approved engine handoff. The contract cannot distinguish another revision, target state, or override set compiled that day.
findings.alternative: Give each plan an immutable identity—such as date plus short source revision and a sequence/nonce—and record it in the schema and filename. Alternatively, retain the date path for an active proposal but fail closed before replacing an approved artifact, requiring a new uniquely named plan.
findings.win: Eliminates silent loss of approved plans and gives the future engine one unambiguous immutable handoff, while preserving in-place regeneration during the proposal consult.
